verbGlossary.CONTAINS: {source} is the parent container that groups {target} as one of its child sections for that project-day, establishing the one-to-many relationship between the log date and all field entries recorded under it.
verbGlossary.CONFIGURES SECTIONS OF: {source} template determines which child sections are required, optional, or hidden within {target}, controlling what data gets collected on each project-day.
verbGlossary.COMPILED INTO: {source} and all its child sections are assembled into {target} as a formatted report package ready for stakeholder distribution.
verbGlossary.LINKS TO TASK VIA: {source} connects to CPM schedule activities through {target}, bridging what was planned in the daily log to what the formal schedule says should happen.
verbGlossary.VERIFIED BY: {source} records the planned subcontractor showing up, and {target} confirms the actual headcount and hours that were worked, closing the plan-vs-actual loop.
verbGlossary.PLANS WORK FOR: {source} allocates a worker or crew to the project for a date range, and {target} records whether that planned labor actually showed up on a given day.
verbGlossary.FILLED BY: {source} signals demand for additional workers, and {target} satisfies that demand by allocating a specific worker or crew to the project.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} is enrolled as a member of {target}, grouping individual workers into the operational crew unit that gets scheduled and managed as a team.
verbGlossary.DEPLOYED THROUGH: {source} is dispatched to project work by being linked to {target}, which defines the date range, project, and scope of the crew's field deployment.
verbGlossary.CLOCKS INTO: {source} is the identity record for the worker, and {target} captures that worker's daily hours, cost code allocation, and overtime classification.
verbGlossary.FEEDS DATA TO: {source} provides the raw daily headcount and trade-level data that {target} aggregates into certified payroll and workforce diversity compliance reports.
verbGlossary.TRACKED DAILY IN: {source} defines the equipment asset identity and specifications, and {target} records that asset's daily presence, operating hours, and idle time on site.
verbGlossary.COSTED THROUGH: {source} defines the equipment asset, and {target} allocates that asset's operating hours to cost codes for equipment cost accounting.
verbGlossary.ARRIVES AS: {source} tracks the inbound shipment from supplier to site, and {target} records the actual arrival event when the material shows up on the ground.
verbGlossary.CONFIRMED BY: {source} logs that a delivery arrived on site, and {target} formally verifies that the delivered quantity, condition, and spec match the purchase order.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: Adverse conditions documented in {source} cause a work stoppage or slowdown that is formally recorded as {target}, establishing the causal link between weather and delay.
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED IN: The structured delay event in {source} is elaborated on with narrative context in {target}, providing the free-text details that the structured fields cannot capture.
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: A safety infraction recorded in {source} results in an injury or more serious event documented in {target}, linking the violation to its consequence.
verbGlossary.DISCOVERS: A field inspection recorded in {source} identifies a safety condition that is then formally cited as {target}, connecting the inspection activity to its enforcement action.
verbGlossary.ITEMIZES LABOR IN: {source} is the parent T&M ticket, and {target} is a labor line item within it detailing worker classification, hours, rate, and cost for that extra work.
verbGlossary.ITEMIZES EQUIPMENT IN: {source} is the parent T&M ticket, and {target} is an equipment line item within it detailing equipment type, hours, rate, and cost for that extra work.
verbGlossary.ITEMIZES MATERIAL IN: {source} is the parent T&M ticket, and {target} is a material line item within it detailing material description, quantity, unit cost, and total for that extra work.
verbGlossary.RECORDS ACTUALS IN: {source} is the field productivity record, and {target} captures the actual quantities produced during the shift as the 'actuals' side of earned value.
verbGlossary.BASELINED BY: {source} is the field productivity record, and {target} provides the planned quantity baseline against which actual production is compared.
verbGlossary.MEASURED AGAINST: {source} captures the actual quantity installed in the field, and {target} provides the budgeted quantity, making the earned-value variance visible.
verbGlossary.FEEDS QUANTITIES TO: {source} records daily installed quantities by cost code, which {target} consumes as the measured field output for earned-value production tracking.
verbGlossary.ROLLS UP INTO: Individual daily entries in {source} are aggregated over time to compute the summary metric in {target}, which provides the project-level or period-level KPI.
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: The operational activity in {source} is the raw input that {target} converts into a standardized rate or percentage metric for benchmarking and comparison.
verbGlossary.NOTED IN: Receipt of updated drawings in {source} is recorded as a narrative entry in {target} to document that the field team received and acknowledged the revision.
verbGlossary.CONFIRMS DELIVERY FOR: {source} documents that material arrived on site, which {target} references when recording the quantity delivered vs. quantity installed for that vendor and scope item.
verbGlossary.LOADED INTO: Waste material recorded in {source} is physically placed into the dumpster tracked by {target}, connecting the waste content record to the container management record.
